heading: Approving States for participation
label: 5703 Approving States for participation

children.heading: Application
children.label: a Application
children.text: Any State may apply to the Secretary for approval to be a participating State under the Program and to be eligible for an allocation of Federal funds under the Program.
a specific department, agency, or political subdivision of the State has been designated to implement a State program and participate in the Program;
all legal actions necessary to enable such designated department, agency, or political subdivision to implement a State program and participate in the Program have been accomplished;

the State has filed an application with the Secretary for approval of a State capital access program under or approval as a State other credit support program under , in each case within the time period provided in the respective section; andsection 5704 of this titlesection 5705 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: conforms to the requirements of this chapter;

children.label: B
children.text: ensures that the State program complies with such national standards as are established by the Secretary under ;section 5708(a)(2) of this title

children.label: C
children.text: sets forth internal control, compliance, and reporting requirements as established by the Secretary, and such other terms and conditions necessary to carry out the purposes of this chapter, including an agreement by the State to allow the Secretary to audit State programs;

children.label: D
children.text: requires that the State program be fully positioned, within 90 days of the State’s execution of the allocation agreement with the Secretary, to act on providing the kind of credit support that the State program was established to provide; and

children.label: E
children.text: includes an agreement by the State to deliver to the Secretary, and update annually, a schedule describing how the State intends to apportion among its State programs the Federal funds allocated to the State.

children.heading: Contractual arrangements for implementation of State programs
children.label: c Contractual arrangements for implementation of State programs

children.text: an existing, approved State program administered by another State; or
an authorized agent of, or entity supervised by, the State, including for-profit and not-for-profit entities.

children.heading: Circumstances when a municipality may apply directly
children.label: 1 Circumstances when a municipality may apply directly
children.text: If a State does not, within 60 days after , file with the Secretary a notice of its intent to apply for approval by the Secretary of a State program or within 9 months after , file with the Secretary a complete application for approval of a State program, the Secretary may grant to municipalities of that State a special permission that will allow them to apply directly to the Secretary without the State for approval to be participating municipalities.March 11, 2021March 11, 2021

children.heading: Timing requirements applicable to municipalities applying directly
children.label: 2 Timing requirements applicable to municipalities applying directly
children.text: To qualify for the special permission, a municipality of a State shall be required, within 12 months after , to file with the Secretary a complete application for approval by the Secretary of a State program.March 11, 2021

children.heading: Notices of intent and applications from more than 1 municipality
children.label: 3 Notices of intent and applications from more than 1 municipality
children.text: A municipality of a State may combine with 1 or more other municipalities of that State to file a joint notice of intent to file and a joint application.

children.heading: Approval criteria
children.label: 4 Approval criteria
children.text: The general approval criteria in paragraphs (2) and (4) shall apply.

children.heading: If more than 3
children.label: A If more than 3
children.text: If more than 3 municipalities, or combination of municipalities as provided in paragraph (3), of a State apply for approval by the Secretary to be participating municipalities under this subsection, and the applications meet the approval criteria in paragraph (4), the Secretary shall allocate Federal funds to the 3 municipalities with the largest populations.

children.heading: If 3 or fewer
children.label: B If 3 or fewer
children.text: If 3 or fewer municipalities, or combination of municipalities as provided in paragraph (3), of a State apply for approval by the Secretary to be participating municipalities under this subsection, and the applications meet the approval criteria in paragraph (4), the Secretary shall allocate Federal funds to each applicant municipality or combination of municipalities.

children.heading: Apportionment of allocated amount among participating municipalities
children.label: 6 Apportionment of allocated amount among participating municipalities
children.text: If the Secretary approves municipalities to be participating municipalities under this subsection, the Secretary shall apportion the full amount of the Federal funds that are allocated to that State to municipalities that are approved under this subsection in amounts proportionate to the population of those municipalities, based on the most recent available decennial census.

children.heading: Approving State programs for municipalities
children.label: 7 Approving State programs for municipalities
children.text: If the Secretary approves municipalities to be participating municipalities under this subsection, the Secretary shall take into account the additional considerations in in making the determination under section 5704 or 5705 of this title that the State program or programs to be implemented by the participating municipalities, including a State capital access program, is eligible for Federal contributions to, or for the account of, the State program.section 5705(d) of this title
